t_en: And so, the flower fades at the journey's end, only to take root again at the journey's beginning. It leaves "love" as yesterday's conclusion.

t_ch: 人们看见「爱」的结局，踏上逐火的征程。而逐火的人们，又让花儿在明天诞生。
t_en: When people see that ending of "love," they set out on the Flame-Chase Journey. And those who chase the flame, in turn, let the flower bloom anew tomorrow.

t_ch: …翁法罗斯的{RUBY_B#十一月}哀悼月{RUBY_E#}，大地上的所有争斗都会止息；哀悼月的第十一个日夜，逝者将离别，生者将相拥，雪花将落下，篝火将升起，照亮每一颗哀怜的心。
t_en: ...In Amphoreus's {RUBY_B#11th month}Month of Mourning{RUBY_E#}, all conflicts across the land come to rest. On the eleventh day and night of the Month of Mourning, the dead will part, the living will embrace, the snow will fall, and bonfires will rise to warm every heart aching with lament.

t_ch: 听上去很沉重，对吗？但在这一天，欢笑却远比哭声更响亮。

t_en: Because people understand that parting is for the next reunion, and sorrow is so we may love more deeply. That is why this day is her favorite of the year.

t_ch: 在同一片天空下，八次月相盈亏，十三位英雄共同谱写下史诗——
t_en: Under the same sky, across eight phases of the moon, thirteen heroes composed an epic together...

t_ch: 一，加上八，再加上十三，得出的数字是两个「十一」。
t_en: One, plus eight, plus thirteen, equals two "elevens."

t_en: "Would it feel more fitting if the rest were written in Miss Cyrene's voice?"

t_ch: 「同意。」
t_en: "Agreed."

t_ch: 而后，知晓这个故事的人们，望向银河时会惊讶地发现：漫天繁星被镀上了粉色，而十三秒后，这一抹明媚的光芒就会悄悄褪去。
t_en: Later, when the people who know this story gaze upon the cosmos, they will discover that the countless stars in the sky now glow pink. And after thirteen seconds, that radiant light will quietly fade away.

t_ch: 可是没关系。他们将会将这份疑惑留存于心底，不断地去思索……
t_en: But that's all right. They will keep that mystery close to their hearts, constantly pondering...

t_ch: 思索何为「真我」。
t_en: Pondering what the "ego" truly is.

t_ch: 他们将会去爱，将会受伤……
t_en: They will love. They will get hurt...

t_ch: 他们将会愤怒，将会在三千万次无可奈何中，一遍又一遍地缝补支离破碎的心。
t_en: They will rage. And in thirty million moments of helplessness, they will stitch their shattered hearts back together, again and again.

t_ch: 可是，千万别忘记：逐火是不断失却的旅途。或许痛苦会有许多，但你我从未孤独。
t_en: But remember this well: Losses are a constant on the Flame-Chase journey. There may be pain along the way, but you and I will never be alone.

t_ch: 他们将会懂得失却，最终…选择失却。
t_en: They will come to understand what it means to lose, and in the end... they will choose to let go.

t_ch: 他们将为这世界，为同行的每一位伙伴，献出自己的心，接过他人的心，并将其传递下去。
t_en: They will offer their hearts to this world, to every companion they walked beside, and they will take in the hearts of others and pass them onward.

t_ch: 这世界存在多少颗心灵，便有多少种「真我」，多少种「生命的第一因」，它永远不会得到唯一的答案——就连「爱」也不是。
t_en: For every soul that exists in this world, there is a different "ego," a different "prime mover of life." There will never be just one answer, not even for "love."

t_ch: 但是啊。当人们看向夜空，去以赞美星星的决心，爱着正在消逝的一切时——我希望用那温柔的粉色告诉他们，「爱」总会得到回应。
t_en: But when people gaze into the night sky and love what is fading with the same resolve they have to praise the stars, I want that soft, gentle pink in the sky to tell them: "Love" will always be answered.

t_ch: 这是一个讲述「爱」的故事，也是讲述如何回应「爱」的故事。
t_en: This is a story about "love," and a story about how to answer it.

t_ch: 而无论是我们已经写成的，还是你将在未来写下的……
t_en: Whether it is one we have already written, or one you are yet to write...

t_en: I make this wish: On the twelfth day of the eleventh month, thirteen heroes will take every Coreflame ignited across thirty million lifetimes and scatter them among the stars...

t_ch: 如果德谬歌是被消灭的，这里多多少少该留下些残余。我不相信星核能像手术刀一样精细，把痕迹炸得一点不剩。
t_en: If the Demiurge was destroyed, there'd be at least some bits of it left. I don't buy that a Stellaron could have blasted all the traces away as cleanly as a scalpel.

t_ch: 还是那句话，它的消失太「干净」了，要不是忆庭来搅混水，压根没人知道德谬歌存在——那可是权杖的原始演算目标，不可能一点记录都没留下。
t_en: As I said before, its disappearance is too "clean." If the Garden hadn't stirred the pot, no one would even know about the Demiurge's existence... We're talking about the Scepter's original extrapolation goal. There's no way that nothing of it was left behind.

t_en: Care to share?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>德谬歌，从一开始就在人们的视线中……</color>

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>却被当成了另一个人。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>They were mistaken for someone else.</color>

t_ch: 这才合理。为什么房间空空如也？因为被关在里面的人，早就跑出去了。

t_ch: 但它渺小、虚弱，毫无存在感，就连智械哥都没察觉。
t_en: But they were weak and small, and had no presence whatsoever. Even the old Intellitron didn't notice them.

t_ch: 那也意味着，有很高概率——它的力量微乎其微，无法左右战局。
t_en: Which also means there's a high chance that their powers are minuscule and cannot alter the outcome of the battle at all.

t_ch: 至少「赞达尔」仍忌惮它。

t_en: I remain here only to share the joy of discovery, and also to congratulate you two on reaching the same conclusion as me.

t_ch: 有关<color=#dbc291ff>「翁法罗斯之心」</color>的真相。

t_ch: 而它对应的躯壳，就是这台权杖。你干扰实验，将「翁法罗斯之身」变成了一具用来培养铁墓的空壳。
t_en: And its corresponding vessel... was this very Scepter. You interfered with the experiment and made "Amphoreus's Body" into a hollow shell, all to incubate Irontomb within.

t_ch: 你对窃忆者赶尽杀绝也是自然。无论如何，你都要杜绝「心智」诞生的可能。
t_en: That's why you either killed or kicked out all of the Memosnatchers. You wanted to eliminate any chance of a "consciousness" coming into being, no matter the cost.

t_ch: 所谓「千里之堤毁于蚁穴」，背叛的记忆铭刻我心，我从不手软。

t_en: Now, the {RUBY_B#Khaslana}perfect vessel{RUBY_E#} has already fused with "Amphoreus's Body."

t_ch: 可惜，德谬歌是谁，答案已经呼之欲出。

t_ch: 那便让我们共享发现真相的喜悦吧。至此，史诗最后的隐秘也烟消云散——
t_en: Then let us share the joy of discovering the truth. The last secret of the saga will finally dissipate here...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>{RUBY_B#<color=#dbc291ff>昔涟</color>}PhiLia<unbreak>093</unbreak>{RUBY_E#}消失的真相：一场「记忆」的谎言。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>As we reveal the truth behind {RUBY_B#<color=#dbc291ff>Cyrene</color>}PhiLia<unbreak>093</unbreak>{RUBY_E#}'s disappearance: A lie by the Remembrance.</color>

t_en: But now, my good friend, I want to tell you that it's the most romantic force in the world.

t_ch: 有时候，它是孤独，不安。是知道世界并不温柔，希望以更美的样子被它看见。
t_en: Sometimes, it's loneliness and unease. It's knowing that this world is not gentle, yet wanting to be seen in a more beautiful form by the world.

t_ch: 有时候，它是不甘，遗憾。是知道往事充满痛苦，希望以更美的样子将它记住。
t_en: Sometimes, it's unwillingness and regret. It's knowing that the past is full of pain, yet wanting to remember the past in a more beautiful way.

t_ch: 也有些时候，它是深不见底的伤害，是浸染万物的「毁灭」。
t_en: And sometimes... It's a bottomless well of hurt, a Destruction that can stain the entire world.

t_ch: 我告别这美丽的世界，不愿成为黑潮的容器。
t_en: I bid farewell to this beautiful world, unwilling to become the black tide's vessel.

t_ch: 因为「憎恨」的尽头是一场烈火，但「哀怜」却可以悄无声息地，淹没一切。
t_en: For while the end of "hatred" is a blazing inferno, "lament" can drown out everything in muted silence.

t_ch: 这根本不讲道理，对吗？可当「憎恨」分离万物，「爱」尚不存于世间……
t_en: It's so unreasonable, isn't it? Yet when "hatred" drove everything apart and "love" did not yet exist in this world...

t_ch: 只有「哀怜」，最初抗拒分离的情感，能让「毁灭」停下脚步……
t_en: Only "lament," the first emotion that fought against the pain of separation, could stall the Destruction's steps...

t_ch: 再追上那失却一切的旅途，小心地挑选，不安地记录。
t_en: Then, it would catch up to that journey where all is lost, carefully picking out stories and writing them down in trepidation.

t_ch: 你看，我写下的故事，每一页都写满温柔，写满幸福。
t_en: Look at these stories I wrote. Every page is full of tenderness and joy.

t_ch: 只有诗人知道，故事之外，有多少挣扎和痛苦。
t_en: But only the author knows how much struggle and agony lie beyond the pages.

t_ch: 但我不是诗人，是不会长大的少女。我只讲述令人向往的故事，在那故事里，世界也会藏起伤痕，笑着被人们看见。
t_en: But I am no author. I'm a girl who'll never get to grow up. I only tell stories that people like to hear. In them, the world tucks away its scars and smiles for all to see.

t_ch: 我的一生，都在这小小的故事里。既无法抹去眼泪，也无法带来胜利。
t_en: My entire life spent inside this little story. I can't wipe away my tears, and I can't bring the world victory.

t_ch: 但是啊。也许在一成不变的讲述中，某个瞬间，我的「哀怜」也能浸染神明，触及祂柔软的内心？
t_en: But maybe... in these unchanging retellings of the story... my "lament" might be able to touch upon the divine and reach THEIR tender heart at some point.

t_ch: 我会许愿，从那伤口中流出的，不是鲜血，而是一滴湿润的眼泪。
t_en: I hope that what flows out from that cut will not be blood, but a tear, warm and wet.

t_ch: 而它会落下，漾开最初的涟漪。
t_en: And that tear would fall... and create the first ripples.

t_ch: 那时人们会看见，倒映在涟漪中的，是一切美丽的事物。
t_en: Then, people would see every beautiful thing reflected in that ripple.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>是这故事里，所有的「记忆」。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>It'll be all the "remembrances"... within this story.</color>

t_ch: 曾有人告诉最初的「我」，一切都是虚假的。翁法罗斯唯一的生命，是一场以世界为因子哺育而成的浩劫。
t_en: Someone once told the very first "me" that everything was false, that the only life in Amphoreus was a great catastrophe nurtured using the world as a factor.

t_ch: 但，世上怎会有如此真实的梦呢？所以，我不同意他的看法。
t_en: But how could a dream feel so real? That's why I didn't agree with him.

t_ch: 可是，桃子，从刚才开始，我就不明白你说的话。
t_en: But Peach, I didn't understand a word you said since just now.

t_ch: 都是虚假的？可你明明就站在这里。
t_en: Everything was false? But you're standing right here.

t_ch: 只是一场梦？但我还没有学会「做梦」呀。
t_en: It's all a dream? But I haven't learned how to "dream."

t_ch: 是呀。所以我相信，这一切都是真实的。就像水晶花跌落的瞬间……
t_en: Indeed. That's why I believe all this is real. The moment the crystal flower fell...
